The institute also built the Arista test‑taking system, an interactive platform designed to evaluate machine understanding through benchmark science tests. In natural language processing, AI2 developed ELMO (Embeddings from Language Models), a deep contextualized word‑representation model that has become a foundational tool for numerous NLP applications.  

In addition to these technical achievements, the institute is known for influential research papers such as “Men Also Like Shopping,” which explores bias in visual reasoning models and has contributed to discussions on fairness in AI. The organization’s research agenda spans foundational AI theory, language understanding, computer vision, and the creation of benchmark datasets and evaluation frameworks that advance the field.  

As a nonprofit, the Allen Institute for AI relies on a combination of philanthropic funding, grants, and collaborations with academic and industry partners. Its leadership, led by Oren Etzioni, emphasizes rigorous, reproducible science and the responsible deployment of AI technologies. Through its diverse projects, publications, and outreach, AI2 plays a pivotal role in shaping the future of AI research and education.

LEE SEDOL.name: LEE SEDOL
LEE SEDOL.description: Lee Sedol is a world‑champion Go player who is frequently cited as an exemplar of human expertise in the game. He reached an expert level after accumulating only tens of thousands of hours of gameplay, demonstrating a remarkable mastery of the ancient board game.

ALLEN INSTITUTE.name: ALLEN INSTITUTE
ALLEN INSTITUTE.description: The Allen Institute for AI (also known as AI2) is a Seattle‑based nonprofit research institute dedicated to advancing the field of artificial intelligence. Founded to conduct high‑impact AI research, the organization is led by Chief Executive Officer Oren Etzioni. Its mission encompasses a broad range of AI‑related topics, including core AI and machine‑learning research, the study of bias in AI systems, ethical and policy considerations, and the societal impact of AI technologies. The institute also publishes scholarly work, such as a paper on “green AI” that appeared in Communications of the ACM, reflecting its commitment to sustainable and efficient AI development. By combining rigorous technical research with interdisciplinary inquiry into AI ethics, policy, and bias, the Allen Institute for AI strives to drive responsible innovation and contribute to the broader AI community.

GREEN AI PAPER.name: GREEN AI PAPER
GREEN AI PAPER.description: The Green AI Paper is the publication by the Allen Institute on green AI, appearing in Communications of the ACM."

ALPHAGO ZERO VS LEE SEDOL.name: ALPHAGO ZERO VS LEE SEDOL
ALPHAGO ZERO VS LEE SEDOL.description: AlphaGo Zero vs Lee Sedol is the historic match where Google DeepMind’s AlphaGo Zero system competed against world‑champion Go player Lee Sedol."

COMMUNICATIONS OF THE ACM.name: COMMUNICATIONS OF THE ACM
COMMUNICATIONS OF THE ACM.description: Communications of the ACM is the academic journal in which the Allen Institute’s green AI paper was published."

GREEN AI.name: GREEN AI
GREEN AI.description: Green AI is a research initiative by the Allen Institute that addresses carbon footprint and efficiency in AI systems, advocating for inclusive research practices"

AI2.name: AI2
AI2.description: AI2, formally known as the Allen Institute for AI, is a nonprofit research institute headquartered in Seattle, Washington, with a mission to advance artificial intelligence for the public good. Founded as a research organization dedicated to high‑impact AI science, AI2 focuses on both foundational and applied research in areas such as natural language processing, machine learning, and scientific data analysis. The institute is widely recognized for developing the ELMo (Embeddings from Language Models) model, a pioneering deep contextualized word‑representation system that has shaped subsequent advances in language understanding. AI2 researchers have also contributed to or collaborated on several other influential language models—including BERT, RoBERTa, and variants of GPT—thereby expanding the institute’s impact across the modern NLP landscape. In parallel with its modeling work, AI2 created the Semantic Scholar platform, an AI‑powered search and recommendation tool that improves access to scientific literature for scholars worldwide. The institute is led by CEO Oren Etzioni, who guides its strategic direction and commitment to open, impactful research. As a nonprofit, AI2 operates through a combination of private funding, academic partnerships, and industry collaborations, and it publishes its findings openly to foster broader scientific progress.

GPT-3 LICENSING.name: GPT-3 LICENSING
GPT-3 LICENSING.description: Microsoft's exclusive licensing of GPT-3, announced recently, grants Microsoft unique rights to the model

AI SAFETY DISCUSSION.name: AI SAFETY DISCUSSION
AI SAFETY DISCUSSION.description: A conversation between Oren Etzioni and Kevin Scott about AI safety, auditing, and responsible deployment

GPT-3 EXCLUSIVE LICENSE.name: GPT-3 EXCLUSIVE LICENSE
GPT-3 EXCLUSIVE LICENSE.description: Microsoft's recent exclusive licensing of GPT-3 from OpenAI, granting Microsoft unique rights to the model)<|COMPLETE|>

DAN WELD.name: DAN WELD
DAN WELD.description: Dan Weld is a professor of computer science and co‑founder of Farecast together with Oren Etzioni.>

ALLEN INSTITUTE OF AI.name: ALLEN INSTITUTE OF AI
ALLEN INSTITUTE OF AI.description: The Allen Institute of AI (also known as AI2) is a Seattle‑based research institute focused on artificial intelligence, led by Oren Etzioni.>

FARECAST.name: FARECAST
FARECAST.description: Farecast is a price‑forecasting company that was co‑founded by Oren Etzioni and Dan Weld. The firm specialized in developing predictive algorithms for airline ticket prices, allowing travelers to anticipate fluctuations and make more informed purchasing decisions. Founded by Etzioni (and later recognized as a joint venture with Weld), Farecast leveraged statistical modeling and historical pricing data to provide forecasts that aimed to help consumers save money on airfare.

MICROSOFT ACADEMIC GRAPH.name: MICROSOFT ACADEMIC GRAPH
MICROSOFT ACADEMIC GRAPH.description: Microsoft Academic Graph is a large academic citation graph created by Microsoft Research, used by Semantic Scholar.>

SEMANTIC SCHOLAR.name: SEMANTIC SCHOLAR
SEMANTIC SCHOLAR.description: Semantic Scholar is an AI‑powered search engine for scientific literature developed by the Allen Institute of AI.>

BROOKLYN BRIDGE.name: BROOKLYN BRIDGE
BROOKLYN BRIDGE.description: The Brooklyn Bridge is a New York City landmark referenced metaphorically in the conversation.>

ALLEN AI LAUNCH.name: ALLEN AI LAUNCH
ALLEN AI LAUNCH.description:
**ALLEN AI LAUNCH** – The Allen Institute for Artificial Intelligence (AI2) was officially launched in 2014. This initiative was conceived and driven by Paul Allen, co‑founder of Microsoft, who envisioned creating an artificial intelligence system capable of reading textbooks and answering questions with a high degree of comprehension. The launch marked the establishment of a dedicated research organization focused on advancing AI in a manner that bridges the gap between raw computational power and deep, human‑like understanding of written material.

AI2’s core mission, articulated at the time of its launch, centers on developing AI technologies that can autonomously parse, understand, and synthesize information from large bodies of text. The institute aims to build systems that not only recognize words and sentences but also grasp the underlying concepts, enabling them to respond to queries in a way that mirrors human expertise. This objective reflects Paul Allen’s belief that AI could become a powerful tool for accelerating scientific discovery and democratizing knowledge by making complex information more accessible.

Since its inception, AI2 has pursued a broad research agenda that includes natural language understanding, machine reading comprehension, and the creation of open‑source resources and datasets to support the wider AI community. The launch in 2014 positioned the institute as a major player in the field, underscoring its commitment to long‑term, high‑impact research rather than short‑term commercial applications.

In summary, the **ALLEN AI LAUNCH** represents the 2014 founding of the Allen Institute for AI, a research enterprise inspired by Paul Allen’s vision to develop an AI capable of reading and comprehending textbooks and answering questions, thereby advancing the frontier of machine reading and knowledge integration.

ARISTA.name: ARISTA
ARISTA.description: Arista is a test-taking system developed by the Allen Institute to benchmark AI performance on science tests, achieving parity with human students.

BERT.name: BERT
BERT.description: BERT is Google's self-supervised language model that was leveraged to improve the Arista system's performance on science tests.

GRADE SCHOOL TESTS.name: GRADE SCHOOL TESTS
GRADE SCHOOL TESTS.description: The benchmarking system using fourth-grade science tests, eighth-grade tests, and SATs to measure AI progress.

ELMO BEST PAPER AWARD 2018.name: ELMO BEST PAPER AWARD 2018
ELMO BEST PAPER AWARD 2018.description: ELMO, an AI model from AI2, won the best paper award in 2018

BERT BEST PAPER AWARD 2019.name: BERT BEST PAPER AWARD 2019
BERT BEST PAPER AWARD 2019.description: BERT, an AI model from Google, won the best paper award in 2019

ELMO MODEL.name: ELMO MODEL
ELMO MODEL.description: ELMO is an AI model developed at AI2 that won the best paper award in 2018

BERT MODEL.name: BERT MODEL
BERT MODEL.description: BERT is Google's AI model that won the best paper award in 2019, named as a nod to ELMO

EP-26_BEHIND_THE_TECH_ORENETZIONI_CEO-AL.name: EP-26_BEHIND_THE_TECH_ORENETZIONI_CEO-ALLENINSTITUTE
EP-26_BEHIND_THE_TECH_ORENETZIONI_CEO-AL.description: A tech talk episode featuring Oren Etzioni, CEO of the Allen Institute for AI, discussing AI and natural language processing>

DECIDE.COM.name: DECIDE.COM
DECIDE.COM.description: Decide.com is a company co-founded by Oren Etzioni

GÖDEL, ESCHER, BACH.name: GÖDEL, ESCHER, BACH
GÖDEL, ESCHER, BACH.description: Gödel, Escher, Bach (often abbreviated as “Gödel, Escher, Bach”) is an influential book written by Douglas Hofstadter. First published in 1979, the work explores the deep interconnections between the mathematics of Kurt Gödel, the art of M.C. Escher, and the music of Johann Sebastian Bach, using these examples to illustrate how self‑reference and formal systems give rise to meaning, consciousness, and the emergent properties of mind. The book has become a seminal text in the fields of artificial intelligence, cognitive science, and philosophy of mind, prompting scholars and technologists alike to reconsider the nature of intelligence, computation, and creativity. Notably, Gödel, Escher, Bach inspired Oren Etzioni, a prominent computer scientist and researcher, to pursue fundamental questions about intelligence and computing, shaping his later contributions to AI. In addition to its scholarly impact, the book has served as a catalyst for public discourse on AI and cognition; it is frequently referenced and discussed during podcasts and media interviews that address the philosophical foundations and future prospects of artificial intelligence. Through its interdisciplinary approach, Gödel, Escher, Bach continues to influence both academic research and broader conversations about the possibilities and limits of machine thought.

TRS-80.name: TRS-80
TRS-80.description: The TRS-80 was an early personal computer that Oren Etzioni discovered as a child, sparking his interest in technology

DOUGLAS HOFSTADTER.name: DOUGLAS HOFSTADTER
DOUGLAS HOFSTADTER.description: Douglas Hofstadter is the author of Gödel, Escher, Bach, a book that influenced Oren Etzioni's intellectual development

MEN ALSO LIKE SHOPPING.name: MEN ALSO LIKE SHOPPING
MEN ALSO LIKE SHOPPING.description:
Men Also Like Shopping is a research paper developed by the Allen Institute for AI (AI2) that earned the best‑paper award at its venue. The work investigates gender bias embedded in large‑scale image collections, demonstrating that automated image‑search systems tend to retrieve a disproportionate number of women when users issue shopping‑related queries. By systematically analyzing search results across multiple shopping categories, the authors reveal a consistent skew toward female‑depicted images, indicating that the underlying datasets encode societal stereotypes. This bias persists even when the queries are neutral, showing that the problem lies in the data rather than in user intent.

The study frames the issue within the broader context of image retrieval bias, highlighting how algorithmic reliance on uncurated visual corpora can perpetuate and amplify existing gender imbalances. Through quantitative experiments and qualitative case studies, the paper quantifies the extent of the disparity and explores its implications for downstream applications such as targeted advertising, e‑commerce, and content recommendation.

Men Also Like Shopping not only uncovers the bias but also proposes methodological guidelines for mitigating such inequities in future dataset construction and retrieval model training. The findings underscore the importance of auditing visual data for gender representation and advocate for more inclusive data‑collection practices to ensure fairness across automated systems.

Overall, Men Also Like Shopping provides a rigorous examination of how gender bias manifests in image datasets used for shopping‑related search, making a compelling case for addressing these disparities to promote equitable outcomes in AI‑driven services.

EU.name: EU
EU.description: The European Union (EU) is a political union that has taken significant steps toward establishing regulatory frameworks for artificial intelligence (AI). One of its notable initiatives is the creation of a “right to explanation” for AI‑driven decisions, which obliges providers and operators of AI systems to give individuals meaningful information about how such decisions are made. This right is intended to increase transparency, accountability, and public trust in automated processes. In addition to this already codified principle, the EU is actively considering broader AI‑specific legislation—such as the proposed EU AI Act—that would further codify and expand the right to explanation, along with other safeguards for high‑risk AI applications. The Union’s approach reflects its commitment to balancing innovation with the protection of fundamental rights, ensuring that AI technologies operate within a clear, enforceable legal environment that respects individual liberties and promotes ethical AI deployment across its member states.

OFFICE FOR RESPONSIBLE AI.name: OFFICE FOR RESPONSIBLE AI
OFFICE FOR RESPONSIBLE AI.description: The Office for Responsible AI is a unit within Microsoft that operates both as a body of the company's legal team and as a division focused on responsible AI practices. Its primary mandate is to ensure that AI technologies developed and deployed by Microsoft adhere to strong ethical standards, guiding policy, review, and risk management across the organization. As part of the legal team, the Office handles AI‑related legal compliance, advising on regulations and mitigating potential harms. Concurrently, as a division overseeing responsible AI, it coordinates cross‑functional initiatives, sets guidelines, and promotes best practices to foster trust, safety, and fairness in AI systems. By integrating legal expertise with strategic oversight, the Office for Responsible AI aims to embed ethical considerations into every stage of AI development and deployment within Microsoft.

AETHER.name: AETHER
AETHER.description: Aether is an AI and ethics committee inside Microsoft

ACLU.name: ACLU
ACLU.description: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to defending and preserving the individual rights and liberties guaranteed by the Constitution and the laws of the United States. In contemporary discussions about the ethical deployment of artificial intelligence, the ACLU is frequently referenced as a civil‑liberties organization that should play a role in auditing AI systems. Proponents argue that, given its expertise in protecting civil rights, the ACLU is well‑positioned to assess AI technologies for potential bias and to ensure that these systems do not infringe upon fundamental freedoms. The organization’s advocacy focus and its legal and policy capacities make it a suitable candidate to examine algorithmic decision‑making processes for fairness and accountability. By conducting such audits, the ACLU could help identify discriminatory patterns, promote transparency, and recommend reforms that align AI development with the core values of civil liberties. This involvement reflects the broader expectation that civil‑rights groups become active participants in AI governance, using their institutional mission to safeguard public interests in an increasingly automated society.

PRESS.name: PRESS
PRESS.description: Press refers to media organizations that can audit AI systems

REGULATORY AGENCIES.name: REGULATORY AGENCIES
REGULATORY AGENCIES.description: Regulatory agencies are governmental bodies that can audit AI systems

PAROLE DECISIONS.name: PAROLE DECISIONS
PAROLE DECISIONS.description: Parole decisions are a part of the criminal justice system where AI may assist

NORTHWEST.name: NORTHWEST
NORTHWEST.description: The Northwest is a region in the United States where Oren Etzioni lives and enjoys outdoor activities such as hiking.>

EP-26.name: EP-26
EP-26.description: EP‑26 is an episode of the “Behind the Tech” podcast series. It is hosted by Kevin Scott and Christina Warren and features Oren Etzioni as the guest. The episode explores topics discussed by Oren Etzioni, with Kevin Scott and Christina Warren guiding the conversation and providing commentary for the audience. This format places Oren Etzioni in the guest role, while Kevin Scott and Christina Warren serve as the primary hosts, ensuring a clear distinction between guest and host responsibilities. EP‑26 thus offers listeners insights from Etzioni’s expertise, framed by the hosts’ questions and contextual remarks.

HOFSTADTER.name: HOFSTADTER
HOFSTADTER.description: HOFSTADTER (Douglas R. Hofstadter) is an American author and cognitive scientist whose work has been referenced in the conversation. He is best known for authoring a book that has had a remarkable intellectual impact on many readers, including the researcher Oren Etzioni, whose own efforts in artificial intelligence have been shaped by Hofstadter’s ideas. In addition to his literary contributions, Hofstadter spent a year or two at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), where he became an influential figure for Etzioni and other scholars interested in the intersections of mind, language, and computation. The book Hofstadter wrote, discussed in the conversation, is cited as a foundational influence that guided Etzioni’s thinking about AI and continues to be regarded as a thought‑provoking work within the cognitive science community.

CHESS.name: CHESS
CHESS.description: CHESS is the classic board game that serves as the focal point in discussions contrasting the richness of human drama with the calculated precision of computer play. Historically, CHESS was regarded as the ultimate test of human intellectual ability, representing the pinnacle of strategic thought and creativity. Before machines were able to master the game, beating a world‑class player was considered an almost insurmountable challenge for artificial intelligence. The eventual triumph of computers over the strongest human players marked a watershed moment, prompting scholars and technologists alike to reconsider what it truly means to possess intelligence. This milestone highlighted how a seemingly purely mental pursuit could be reduced to algorithmic processes, raising questions about the nature of cognition, creativity, and the boundaries between human and machine expertise.

GO.name: GO
GO.description: Go, the ancient Eastern board game, is frequently cited alongside chess as a benchmark of high human intellectual achievement that artificial intelligence systems have successfully mastered. Researchers and commentators highlight Go as a second, equally demanding domain where machines can reach expert‑level performance. In particular, AI programs have demonstrated champion‑level proficiency in Go, matching or surpassing the best human players in competitive settings. This dual reference underscores the significance of Go as a challenging environment for testing and showcasing the capabilities of modern machine‑learning algorithms, illustrating how AI can achieve elite performance across diverse strategic games.

GPT-3.name: GPT-3
GPT-3.description: GPT‑3 is a large language model developed by OpenAI and represents the third generation of the company’s series of generative pre‑trained models. It was first released to the public through an API, a deployment approach that marked a significant breakthrough in how advanced AI systems are made accessible to developers and researchers. The model’s scale and capability have been highlighted by experts such as Oren Etzioni, who cite GPT‑3 as a striking example of the rapidly increasing power of AI technologies. By providing powerful language understanding and generation via a simple API interface, GPT‑3 lowered barriers to experimentation and enabled a wide range of applications, from natural‑language reasoning to creative writing and code generation. Its introduction underscored a shift in the AI landscape, demonstrating that models with tens of billions of parameters could be deployed at scale and used in production environments, thereby setting new benchmarks for subsequent generations of large language models.

ARTIFICIAL GENERAL INTELLIGENCE.name: ARTIFICIAL GENERAL INTELLIGENCE
ARTIFICIAL GENERAL INTELLIGENCE.description:
Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) refers to a hypothetical form of artificial intelligence that would be capable of performing any intellectual task that a human can do, mirroring the full breadth of human cognition across diverse domains. Unlike current AI systems, which are designed for narrow, well‑defined tasks such as image recognition or language translation, AGI envisions a machine that can understand, learn, and apply knowledge in a flexible, context‑aware manner. This ambition positions AGI as the ultimate goal of many AI research initiatives, representing a level of machine intelligence that would surpass the limitations of today’s specialized algorithms.

While some researchers and enthusiasts argue that existing AI technologies are on the cusp of achieving AGI, Bill Gates and other skeptics contend that this view is premature. They maintain that contemporary AI models, despite impressive performance in specific areas, still lack the general reasoning, common‑sense understanding, and adaptive learning capabilities required for true AGI. Consequently, the prevailing consensus among many experts is that AGI remains a long‑term, speculative objective rather than an imminent reality.

In summary, Artificial General Intelligence denotes a future AI system capable of human‑level cognition across all domains, distinct from the narrow‑focused AI that dominates the current landscape. The realization of AGI continues to be a subject of intense debate, with differing opinions on how close the field is to turning this vision into a tangible achievement.

COMMON SENSE REASONING.name: COMMON SENSE REASONING
COMMON SENSE REASONING.description: Common sense reasoning is identified as something that remains elusive for machines despite AI progress in other areas

FACIAL RECOGNITION.name: FACIAL RECOGNITION
FACIAL RECOGNITION.description: Facial recognition is cited as another increasingly powerful AI technological capability

ZIONI.name: ZIONI
ZIONI.description: Zioni is a host/interviewer in a technology podcast discussing AI and intelligence

MORAVEC.name: MORAVEC
MORAVEC.description: Moravec is referenced as the thinker who articulated the paradox about machine and human capabilities

TURING TEST.name: TURING TEST
TURING TEST.description: The Turing Test is a historic benchmark for evaluating machine intelligence, originally conceived as a means to determine whether a machine’s behavior can be indistinguishable from that of a human when interacted with by a human evaluator. In modern discussions it is frequently cited as a landmark yardstick that contemporary AI systems have already surpassed, reflecting dramatic advances in language understanding, reasoning, and interactive capabilities. Although the test remains a conceptual touchstone for assessing whether a machine can convince a human that it is human, critics note that it measures only superficial conversational mimicry rather than deeper comprehension. Nevertheless, the Turing Test continues to serve as a reference point for AI evaluation, highlighting both its historical significance and the extent to which recent AI achievements have moved beyond the original criterion.

MORAVEC'S PARADOX.name: MORAVEC'S PARADOX
MORAVEC'S PARADOX.description: Moravec's Paradox describes the observation that tasks easy for humans (like common sense) are difficult for machines, while tasks easy for machines (like chess) are difficult for humans

HOWARD LEWIS.name: HOWARD LEWIS
HOWARD LEWIS.description: Howard Lewis is a Harvard professor who signed Oren Etzioni's concentration form, making him the first computer science major at Harvard

MASSACHUSETTS.name: MASSACHUSETTS
MASSACHUSETTS.description: Massachusetts is a U.S. state situated in the New England region of the United States. It is notably home to the city of Cambridge, which houses the world‑renowned Harvard University, establishing the state as a major hub of higher education. Massachusetts also plays a role in the personal history of physician and astronaut Mae Jemison: her father attended a trade school located in the state, and after leaving high school Mae herself attended a trade school in Massachusetts. These connections highlight Massachusetts as a place where both higher academic institutions and vocational training have influenced the lives of notable individuals.

EP-26 BEHIND THE TECH.name: EP-26 BEHIND THE TECH
EP-26 BEHIND THE TECH.description: EP-26 Behind the Tech is a podcast episode featuring Kevin Scott interviewing Oren Etzioni about AI and the history of computer science

MICHAEL RABIN.name: MICHAEL RABIN
MICHAEL RABIN.description: Michael Rabin is a Turing Award winner who taught theoretical computer science at Harvard and influenced Oren Etzioni

LES VALIANT.name: LES VALIANT
LES VALIANT.description: Les Valiant taught combinatorics at Harvard and was part of the mathematical curriculum that influenced Oren Etzioni

TOM MITCHELL.name: TOM MITCHELL
TOM MITCHELL.description: Tom Mitchell is a pioneering computer scientist often called the father of machine learning, author of a classic textbook covering statistical methods and Bayesian inference, and Oren Etzioni’s PhD advisor

INTERNET REVOLUTION.name: INTERNET REVOLUTION
INTERNET REVOLUTION.description: The Internet Revolution refers to the global expansion of internet technology that fundamentally changed how people communicate, access information, and conduct business. It marks a period of rapid growth in internet companies, accompanied by the development of distributed computing and cloud infrastructure, which later laid the groundwork for big‑data analytics and deep‑learning breakthroughs. Throughout this transformation, Bill Gates played a significant role by leveraging his influence and resources to accelerate the adoption of internet‑based services and to champion the technological shifts that defined the era. The widespread growth and adoption of the internet that characterized the revolution reshaped society, enabling new forms of collaboration, commerce, and knowledge sharing on an unprecedented scale.

GEOFF.name: GEOFF
GEOFF.description: Geoff is a person who, according to Oren Etzioni, is noted for being both stubborn and persistent in his advocacy of an idea concerning distributed computing and cloud infrastructure. Etzioni highlights Geoff’s determination in promoting this concept, emphasizing that these traits were evident when the idea was originally presented.

EARLY 1990S CMU DISCUSSION.name: EARLY 1990S CMU DISCUSSION
EARLY 1990S CMU DISCUSSION.description: The “EARLY 1990S CMU DISCUSSION” refers to a notable conversation that took place at Carnegie Mellon University in the early 1990s. The discussion centered on the topics of cognitive architecture and artificial intelligence, bringing together several researchers from the CMU community. Oren Etzioni was among the participants, along with other scholars interested in exploring the foundations and design principles of intelligent systems. This exchange contributed to the ongoing dialogue about how computational models could emulate or inform human‑like cognition, reflecting the interdisciplinary nature of AI research at that time.

AGI.name: AGI
AGI.description: AGI refers to Artificial General Intelligence, a topic Oren Etzioni mentions wanting to discuss.'

LARGE-SCALE COMPUTE INFRASTRUCTURE PROJE.name: LARGE-SCALE COMPUTE INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECT
LARGE-SCALE COMPUTE INFRASTRUCTURE PROJE.description: Large-scale compute infrastructure that is currently being built and represents something that may not reach AGI but will achieve something significant.'

2020 YEAR IN REVIEW.name: 2020 YEAR IN REVIEW
2020 YEAR IN REVIEW.description: 2020 Year In Review is a special episode of the Behind the Tech podcast that recaps the most significant events and conversations from 2020. It is officially titled EP‑27 Behind the Tech 2020 Year In Review and is hosted by Christina Warren and Kevin Scott. The episode features author Charlie Stross as a guest speaker and revisits a selection of the year’s discussions, focusing on topics such as artificial intelligence, synthetic biology, and other technological trends that shaped the period. While the label “2020 Year In Review” could suggest a multi‑episode series, the primary release is a single episode that serves as a comprehensive roundup of the year’s content on the podcast. This episode is part of the broader Behind the Tech series, offering listeners a curated look back at the key themes, breakthroughs, and perspectives explored throughout the year.

THE PRINCESS BRIDE.name: THE PRINCESS BRIDE
THE PRINCESS BRIDE.description: The Princess Bride is a classic film referenced by Kevin Scott when discussing the use of the word "unprecedented" in 2020

DR. DREW ENDY.name: DR. DREW ENDY
DR. DREW ENDY.description: Dr. Drew Enny is a Stanford professor and bioengineer mentioned at the beginning of the discussion

DR. TOM DANIEL.name: DR. TOM DANIEL
DR. TOM DANIEL.description: Dr. Tom Daniel is a neuroscientist and bioengineer who serves as a professor at the University of Washington. His research integrates neuroscience, engineering, computing, and biomechanics to elucidate how biological systems control movement. He explores advanced technologies such as microfabrication, electrode technology, machine learning, and gene editing to probe neural mechanisms and develop innovative tools for neuroscience. Through this interdisciplinary approach, Dr. Daniel aims to deepen the understanding of motor control in living organisms and advance applications in biomedical engineering.

FLIES.name: FLIES
FLIES.description: Flies are insects discussed in the context of Tom Daniel's research on halteres and neurobiology

2020 YEAR-IN-REVIEW.name: 2020 YEAR-IN-REVIEW
2020 YEAR-IN-REVIEW.description: The 2020 Year‑In‑Review is an event or series that provides a comprehensive look at the year’s technology developments, and it also serves as an episode theme that highlights the most significant technology advances of 2020. As an event, it gathers key insights, trends, breakthroughs, and notable moments across the technology landscape, offering audiences a curated overview of how the industry evolved over the year. As an episode theme, it frames the discussion around the major advancements, breakthroughs, and challenges that defined the technology sector during 2020, delivering focused commentary and analysis on those topics. The overall purpose of the 2020 Year‑In‑Review is to inform, reflect, and celebrate the progress made in technology while providing a cohesive summary of the year’s achievements and shifts.

EP-27.name: EP-27
EP-27.description:
EP-27 is episode number 27 of the Behind the Tech podcast series. This specific episode is titled "2020 Year In Review" and serves as a comprehensive discussion reflecting on the events and developments of 2020 within the technology realm. The episode features conversations with notable guests including Kevin Scott, Christina Warren, and Tom Daniel, who share their insights and perspectives on the year's significant technological advancements, challenges, and trends.

EP-27 represents the Behind the Tech podcast's annual tradition of reviewing the past year's most important technology stories and discussions. As an identifier, EP-27 is used to reference this particular episode within the broader Behind the Tech series, making it easily identifiable for listeners who wish to access this specific content. The episode encapsulates the highlights and key moments from the podcast's coverage throughout 2020, offering audiences a consolidated look back at the technological landscape during that period.

The episode brings together voices from across the technology community, providing diverse viewpoints on how various developments and events shaped the industry. Through its format, EP-27 allows listeners to revisit or catch up on important conversations that may have occurred throughout the year, serving as both an educational resource and a retrospective for the Behind the Tech audience. The designation EP-27 helps organize the podcast's library, enabling proper cataloging and easy retrieval of this particular installment among the series' growing collection of episodes.

DREW.name: DREW
DREW.description: Individual mentioned by Kevin Scott as a participant in the conversation about biology

BEHIND THE TECH 2020 YEAR-IN-REVIEW.name: BEHIND THE TECH 2020 YEAR-IN-REVIEW
BEHIND THE TECH 2020 YEAR-IN-REVIEW.description:
The Behind the Tech 2020 Year‑In‑Review is a special episode of the “Behind the Tech” podcast, designated episode EP‑27, that looks back at the most significant technology developments of 2020. The episode is anchored by Kevin Scott, who is joined by co‑host Christina Warren for the bulk of the conversation, with occasional contributions from Greg Shaw, reflecting the show’s collaborative format.  

Among the guests featured are science‑fiction author Charlie Stross, AI researcher Daphne Koller, technologists Dr. Tom Daniel and Dr. Oren Etzioni, and Microsoft executive Eric Horvitz, among other technology professionals. The discussions span a wide range of topics, including the intersection of machine learning and biology, advances in artificial intelligence, ethical considerations in AI, and broader trends shaping the tech landscape. By gathering voices from academia, industry, and the arts, the episode provides a comprehensive overview of how technology evolved throughout the year.  

Overall, the Behind the Tech 2020 Year‑In‑Review offers listeners a concise yet wide‑ranging recap of the innovations, challenges, and conversations that defined the tech world in 2020.

EP-27_ BEHIND_THE_TECH_2020-YEAR-IN-REVI.name: EP-27_ BEHIND_THE_TECH_2020-YEAR-IN-REVIEW
EP-27_ BEHIND_THE_TECH_2020-YEAR-IN-REVI.description: EP-27 Behind the Tech 2020 Year in Review is a podcast episode featuring Kevin Scott and Oren Etzioni discussing AI capabilities and policy

AITHER.name: AITHER
AITHER.description: AITHER is Microsoft's AI and ethics committee

BEHIND THE TECH 2020-YEAR-IN-REVIEW.name: BEHIND THE TECH 2020-YEAR-IN-REVIEW
BEHIND THE TECH 2020-YEAR-IN-REVIEW.description: Behind the Tech 2020-Year-In-Review is a podcast episode featuring discussions on AI ethics

KEVIN SCOTT BOOK LAUNCH.name: KEVIN SCOTT BOOK LAUNCH
KEVIN SCOTT BOOK LAUNCH.description: The Kevin Scott Book Launch is the event marking the release of Kevin Scott’s book "Reprogramming the American Dream: From Rural America to Silicon Valley – Making AI Serve Us All," during which he reads an excerpt.>

EP-27_BEHIND_THE_TECH_2020-YEAR-IN-REVIE.name: EP-27_BEHIND_THE_TECH_2020-YEAR-IN-REVIEW
EP-27_BEHIND_THE_TECH_2020-YEAR-IN-REVIE.description: EP‑27_BEHIND_THE_TECH_2020‑YEAR‑IN‑REVIEW is a podcast episode that features a candid conversation between Kevin Scott, Microsoft’s Chief Technology Officer, and Greg Shaw, a technology journalist and commentator. In this episode, they explore the theme of AI accessibility and discuss how advances in artificial intelligence can impact the broader concept of the American Dream. While the discussion touches on the aspirational promise of AI for all Americans, a significant portion of the dialogue focuses on the specific implications for rural America, where connectivity, education, and economic opportunities have historically lagged behind urban centers. Scott and Shaw examine how accessible AI tools can empower individuals in rural communities, create new pathways for entrepreneurship, and help bridge the opportunity gap that has long defined the American experience. The conversation also reflects on the responsibilities of technology leaders to ensure that AI development is inclusive, equitable, and aligned with the values that underpin the American Dream. Throughout the episode, the hosts highlight real‑world examples and potential future scenarios where AI could serve as a catalyst for positive social change, especially for those living in underserved regions. By intertwining the broad notion of the American Dream with the tangible challenges faced by rural populations, EP‑27_BEHIND_THE_TECH_2020‑YEAR‑IN‑REVIEW offers a nuanced look at how AI accessibility can shape the future of opportunity in the United States.

BEHIND THE TECH 2020 YEAR IN REVIEW.name: BEHIND THE TECH 2020 YEAR IN REVIEW
BEHIND THE TECH 2020 YEAR IN REVIEW.description:
**BEHIND THE TECH 2020 YEAR IN REVIEW** is Episode 27 of the *Behind the Tech* podcast series. The episode provides a comprehensive year‑end recap of the most significant technological and scientific developments of 2020, weaving together insights from leading technologists, researchers, and innovators. 

Key topics explored in the episode include breakthroughs in biotechnology—such as advances in gene editing, vaccine platforms, and biomedical diagnostics—alongside a deep dive into the rapid evolution of artificial‑intelligence (AI) capabilities. The discussion highlights how AI can be harnessed to improve humanity, examining ethical considerations, applications in health care, and the role of AI in shaping future societal outcomes. 

The program also covers the Nobel Prize recognitions that were awarded in 2020, explaining the impact of those achievements on the broader scientific landscape and how they intersect with emerging technologies. Through conversations with prominent experts, the episode offers perspectives on the future directions of technology, addressing trends such as quantum computing, edge computing, sustainability, and the convergence of multiple scientific disciplines. 

Listeners are guided through a narrative that connects the year’s milestones to the overarching mission of the *Behind the Tech* podcast: to make complex tech topics accessible and to inspire thoughtful dialogue about the role of innovation in shaping a better world. The episode synthesizes technical detail with broader societal implications, providing a balanced view of both the promises and challenges that lie ahead. 

Overall, **BEHIND THE TECH 2020 YEAR IN REVIEW** serves as a concise yet thorough chronicle of 2020’s tech and science landscape, delivering expert analysis, memorable anecdotes, and forward‑looking insights for anyone interested in understanding how technology evolves and how it can be steered to benefit humanity.

2020.description: The year 2020 is discussed in the episode as a significant period marked by the global pandemic and rapid scientific progress. It is referenced as the year drawing to a close, suggesting a retrospective perspective on this particular time period. The episode covers the events and developments that defined 2020, highlighting how the year was characterized by unprecedented challenges alongside notable advancements in scientific understanding and innovation.

HALTING STATE.name: HALTING STATE
HALTING STATE.description: Halting State is a science fiction novel by Charlie Stross inspired by MMO economies

RULE 34.name: RULE 34
RULE 34.description: Rule 34 is a science fiction novel by Charlie Stross, part of a planned trilogy

CAPTURE THE FLAG GAME.name: CAPTURE THE FLAG GAME
CAPTURE THE FLAG GAME.description: An ongoing competition between rival teams of Chinese hackers trying to hijack the economy of a small European state within the MMO context of the novel

DEAD LIES DREAMING RELEASE.name: DEAD LIES DREAMING RELEASE
DEAD LIES DREAMING RELEASE.description: The October release of Charlie Stross's novel Dead Lies Dreaming, mentioned by Kevin Scott

HAPPY SLAPPING PHENOMENON.name: HAPPY SLAPPING PHENOMENON
HAPPY SLAPPING PHENOMENON.description: A short‑lived craze among teenagers where they filmed assaults and posted them on YouTube

2006.description: Year referenced when discussing predictability of technology standards and cellular networks

2007.description: Year referenced when discussing predictability of technology standards and cellular networks

2017.description: Year referenced when discussing technology predictions, including 3G and 4G network availability

3G CELLULAR TELEPHONY.name: 3G CELLULAR TELEPHONY
3G CELLULAR TELEPHONY.description: Third-generation wireless technology standard that was predicted to be standard by 2017

CCD IMAGE SENSOR.name: CCD IMAGE SENSOR
CCD IMAGE SENSOR.description: Technology developed in the 1980s that enabled cheap, high-quality cameras in modern devices

GOOGLE GLASS.name: GOOGLE GLASS
GOOGLE GLASS.description: Augmented reality wearable product by Google that crashed in the market for social reasons rather than technological reasons

EP-27 BEHIND THE TECH 2020 YEAR IN REVIE.name: EP-27 BEHIND THE TECH 2020 YEAR IN REVIEW
EP-27 BEHIND THE TECH 2020 YEAR IN REVIE.description: EP-27 is a specific episode of the Behind the Tech podcast titled 2020 Year In Review featuring Kevin Scott and Eric Horvitz

DR. ERIC HORVITZ.name: DR. ERIC HORVITZ
DR. ERIC HORVITZ.description: Dr. Eric Horvitz is the Chief Scientific Officer at Microsoft, known for research in AI, biology, and high‑performance computing.>

MS. COHEN.name: MS. COHEN
MS. COHEN.description: Ms. Cohen is an educator who received a shout‑out from the hosts during the episode.

DR. TOM MORGAN.name: DR. TOM MORGAN
DR. TOM MORGAN.description: Dr. Tom Morgan taught Kevin Scott computer science at the Central Virginia Governor's School

DR. PERCY LIANG.name: DR. PERCY LIANG
DR. PERCY LIANG.description: Dr. Percy Liang is an associate professor of computer science at Stanford University, a guest on the show, and a classical pianist who enjoys late Beethoven sonatas

CENTRAL VIRGINIA GOVERNOR'S SCHOOL.name: CENTRAL VIRGINIA GOVERNOR'S SCHOOL
CENTRAL VIRGINIA GOVERNOR'S SCHOOL.description: The Central Virginia Governor's School is where Dr. Tom Morgan taught Kevin Scott computer science

THE PROMS.name: THE PROMS
THE PROMS.description: The Proms is an annual classical music festival staged in London that showcases a wide range of orchestral and solo performances throughout the summer. The festival has long been recognized for its accessibility, offering nightly concerts at prestigious venues such as the Royal Albert Hall, and for its commitment to presenting both established and emerging artists. One notable event in the festival’s history featured the renowned pianist Martha Argerich performing Franz Liszt’s Piano Concerto No. 2, highlighting the event’s ability to attract world‑class soloists. The combination of its historic tradition, diverse programming, and high‑profile guest artists underscores The Proms’ role as a cornerstone of the London cultural calendar and a major venue for classical music excellence.

ALLEN AI INSTITUTE.name: ALLEN AI INSTITUTE
ALLEN AI INSTITUTE.description: Allen AI Institute is a research institute focused on developing powerful artificial intelligence systems to help solve large‑scale problems on the planet.

G MINOR BALADE.name: G MINOR BALADE
G MINOR BALADE.description: Chopin's G Minor Ballade is a classical music composition that Kevin Scott describes as provoking intense emotional reactions and goosebumps during certain performances

2021.description: 2021 is characterized as the year that captures the tail‑end “energy” of the boom times, especially within the technology sector, marking it as the final phase of a period of rapid growth and heightened optimism. It is also framed as a year for hopes and future plans, a time when individuals and organizations look ahead and set new objectives. This dual portrayal positions 2021 as a transitional point—still riding the momentum of a boom—contrasted with the more resource‑conscious approach that is expected to define 2022.

DAPHNE.name: DAPHNE
DAPHNE.description: Daphne is the individual whose genetics are used to create a neuron in the described experiments; the derived neuron is referred to as a Daphne neuron.

BEHIND_THE_TECH_2020_YEAR_IN_REVIEW.name: BEHIND_THE_TECH_2020_YEAR_IN_REVIEW
BEHIND_THE_TECH_2020_YEAR_IN_REVIEW.description: Behind the Tech 2020 Year In Review is an episode (EP‑27) that reviews technological advances, highlighting digital biology, stem‑cell research, and CRISPR applications.

INDUCED_PLURIPOTENT_STEM_CELLS.name: INDUCED_PLURIPOTENT_STEM_CELLS
INDUCED_PLURIPOTENT_STEM_CELLS.description: Induced pluripotent stem cells is a technology that allows skin or blood cells to be reverted to an embryonic state, where they can differentiate into any cell lineage, enabling personalized disease modeling.

DIGITAL_BIOLOGY.name: DIGITAL_BIOLOGY
DIGITAL_BIOLOGY.description: Digital biology is an emerging field that combines advanced data production capabilities with machine learning interpretation to quantitatively measure, understand, and modify biological systems.

NOBEL PRIZE FOR CHEMISTRY.name: NOBEL PRIZE FOR CHEMISTRY
NOBEL PRIZE FOR CHEMISTRY.description: The Nobel Prize for Chemistry is an award given in 2020 to two women for the development of CRISPR gene‑editing technology.>

SARS-COV-2.name: SARS-COV-2
SARS-COV-2.description: The COVID-19 virus pandemic, discussed as an example of current biological threats

LEGO.name: LEGO
LEGO.description: The LEGO Group is a Danish toy manufacturer that created the Women NASA Lego kit featuring Dr. Mae Jemison.

HALIFU OSUMARE.name: HALIFU OSUMARE
HALIFU OSUMARE.description: Halifu Osumare is a professor of dance at Stanford University. During Mae Jemison’s time at Stanford, Osumare served as her dance instructor and mentor, guiding her in dance technique while also fostering her confidence and belief in her abilities as an engineer and scientist. Osumare’s encouragement helped Jemison develop a sense of self‑assurance that complemented her scientific pursuits. By integrating dance with personal empowerment, Osumare played a formative role in both Jemison’s artistic growth and her broader academic confidence.

ARTEMIS.name: ARTEMIS
ARTEMIS.description: NASA’s Artemis program is a major initiative dedicated to returning humans to the Moon. As part of this effort, the program has announced a list of potential crew members who could take part in future lunar missions, underscoring its commitment to sending astronauts back to the lunar surface under the Artemis banner.

PEACE CORPS.name: PEACE CORPS
PEACE CORPS.description: U.S. volunteer organization that operates worldwide, including in Sierra Leone and Liberia, where Mae Jemison served as Area Medical Officer

FLYING DOCTORS.name: FLYING DOCTORS
FLYING DOCTORS.description: Medical humanitarian group that provided services in East Africa, where Mae Jemison worked

THE EARTH WE SHARE.name: THE EARTH WE SHARE
THE EARTH WE SHARE.description:
The Earth We Share is an international experiential science education program created by former astronaut and physician Dr. Mae Jemison in 1994. The initiative is housed under the Dorothy Jemison Foundation for Excellence and is designed to engage young people in solving complex global challenges while emphasizing sustainable living on Earth. Originally a four‑week residential camp, the program has expanded to include day‑long workshops and a summer partnership with the Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD), making it accessible to a broader audience of students worldwide.

The Earth We Share targets adolescents between the ages of 12 and 16, inviting them to tackle issues such as climate change, population growth, resource management, waste reduction, predicting future stocks, designing sustainable housing, and determining Earth’s carrying capacity. Through hands‑on, experiential learning activities, participants develop critical thinking, collaboration, and problem‑solving skills. The program also integrates teacher training, equipping educators with tools to deliver experiential curricula in their own classrooms.

By fostering a global community of young learners, The Earth We Share aims to inspire the next generation of leaders to think systemically about the planet’s future and to implement viable, sustainable solutions. The program’s blend of scientific inquiry, real‑world application, and interdisciplinary collaboration reflects Dr. Mae Jemison’s vision of education as a catalyst for positive global change.

SIERRA LEONE.name: SIERRA LEONE
SIERRA LEONE.description: West African country where Mae Jemison worked as a Peace Corps Medical Officer

LIBERIA.name: LIBERIA
LIBERIA.description: West African country where Mae Jemison worked as a Peace Corps Medical Officer

EAST AFRICA.name: EAST AFRICA
EAST AFRICA.description: Region in Africa where Mae Jemison worked with the Flying Doctors

CAMBODIA.name: CAMBODIA
CAMBODIA.description: Southeast Asian country where Mae Jemison worked in a refugee camp

THE MOON.name: THE MOON
THE MOON.description: Earth's natural satellite, a destination for NASA missions and future human exploration

FUSION.name: FUSION
FUSION.description: Fusion refers to the global effort to develop viable fusion energy as a potential power source. This endeavor encompasses the research and development of nuclear fusion technology, which scientists and companies worldwide are working to advance as a means of generating clean, abundant energy. Helion is among the organizations actively pursuing fusion technology as part of this broader initiative to achieve sustainable energy solutions through nuclear fusion.

ANTIMATTER.name: ANTIMATTER
ANTIMATTER.description: Antimatter technology that could provide massive energy resources if containment challenges are solved

MICROBIOME.name: MICROBIOME
MICROBIOME.description: Scientific research area focusing on understanding microorganisms in the human body

SPACE.name: SPACE
SPACE.description:
**SPACE** refers to the vast expanse beyond Earth's atmosphere, encompassing the solar system and all celestial bodies located throughout the universe. This domain extends far beyond our planet, including planets, stars, moons, asteroids, comets, galaxies, and other cosmic phenomena that exist in the infinite void. The concept of space has fascinated humanity for centuries, representing both a scientific frontier and an realm of endless exploration potential.

Within the context of modern technology and connectivity, **SPACE** serves as an intriguing domain for bridging the gap between ordinary people and the extraordinary possibilities of space travel and cosmic discovery. The **Skyfie app** utilizes this fascination by leveraging space as a central theme to connect individuals with the wonders of the universe. Through this application, users can engage with content related to space exploration, astronomical phenomena, and the broader cosmos in an accessible and interactive manner.

The integration of space into the Skyfie platform demonstrates how ancient human curiosity about the stars can be channeled through contemporary digital tools to foster greater public interest and understanding of space-related topics. Whether discussing the technical aspects of space travel, the beauty of distant galaxies, or the scientific principles governing celestial bodies, the app positions space as an inclusive topic that brings people together through shared wonder and learning.

By framing space within the context of connectivity and community, the Skyfie app transforms what might seem like an inaccessible subject into something tangible and engaging for everyday users. **SPACE** thus becomes not merely a scientific concept but also a unifying theme that connects people across the globe through their mutual interest in exploring the universe beyond Earth.

PLAN B.name: PLAN B
PLAN B.description: The concept of space exploration as an alternative to solving Earth's problems

AFRICAN STUDIES.name: AFRICAN STUDIES
AFRICAN STUDIES.description: Academic discipline that Mae Jemison majored in during college

EPIDEMIOLOGY.name: EPIDEMIOLOGY
EPIDEMIOLOGY.description: Branch of medicine that studies disease distribution and determinants in populations

HEALTH PROGRAMMING.name: HEALTH PROGRAMMING
HEALTH PROGRAMMING.description: Educational initiatives that Mae Jemison taught during her Peace Corps service

REFUGEE CAMP.name: REFUGEE CAMP
REFUGEE CAMP.description: Temporary settlement for displaced people in Cambodia where Mae Jemison worked

LEVAR BURTON.name: LEVAR BURTON
LEVAR BURTON.description: LeVar Burton is an American actor, educator, and television host. He is best known for his roles on television and as the host of the children’s series *Reading Rainbow*. In addition to his work in entertainment, Burton has been actively involved in educational initiatives; he co‑created the Look Up, One Sky program, which encourages public engagement with science and the cosmos. He also serves on the advisory board of the 100 Year Starship, an organization dedicated to ensuring humanity’s capability for interstellar travel within the next century. Through these efforts, Burton continues to blend his passion for storytelling, learning, and scientific exploration, making a lasting impact both on screen and in the broader community.

JILL TARTER.name: JILL TARTER
JILL TARTER.description: Jill Tarter is an American astronomer who served as former director of the Center for SETI Research, a division of the SETI Institute, and also held the position of former director of the broader SETI Institute itself. In addition to her leadership at the Institute, she co‑created the “Look Up, One Sky” initiative, a program dedicated to engaging the public in the wonder of the night sky. Tarter continues to contribute to interstellar exploration as a member of the advisory board of the 100 Year Starship project, an effort aimed at advancing human travel beyond the solar system within the next century. Her career blends scientific research, public outreach, and strategic planning to promote the search for extraterrestrial intelligence and the long‑term goal of interstellar travel.

SOUTH BY SOUTHWEST.name: SOUTH BY SOUTHWEST
SOUTH BY SOUTHWEST.description: South by Southwest (SXSW) is an annual festival and conference held in Austin, Texas. The event gained recognition for premiering the 100 Year Starship initiative, which represents a significant scientific endeavor focused on interstellar travel capabilities within the next century. The premiere of this initiative at SXSW brought together notable figures from the scientific and entertainment communities, including LeVar Burton, Jill Tarter, and additional speakers who contributed to discussions surrounding the ambitious project. The conference provided a platform for presenting this futuristic concept to a broader audience, combining technology, science, and innovative thinking within the vibrant atmosphere that characterizes the SXSW festival. The presence of these distinguished individuals highlighted the intersection of scientific research and public engagement, demonstrating how events like SXSW serve to bridge complex scientific initiatives with public awareness and interest. The festival's location in Austin, Texas, creates an ideal environment for such innovative presentations, drawing participants from diverse backgrounds who gather to explore emerging ideas and technologies across multiple disciplines.

LOOK UP, ONE SKY.name: LOOK UP, ONE SKY
LOOK UP, ONE SKY.description: Look Up, One Sky is an initiative related to 100 Year Starship that promotes connection to the sky and space for everyone

SKYFIE APP.name: SKYFIE APP
SKYFIE APP.description: Skyfie App (often simply called Skyfie) is the software platform and mobile application created as part of the Look Up, One Sky initiative. It serves as a digital hub that enables users to submit and view sky‑related content on an interactive globe, fostering a shared experience of the sky and space. By combining the functionality of a globe‑based interface with the accessibility of a mobile app, Skyfie helps people connect with the sky and outer space, encouraging exploration, observation, and community engagement around atmospheric and celestial phenomena.

LOOK UP ONE SKY.name: LOOK UP ONE SKY
LOOK UP ONE SKY.description: Look Up, One Sky is a global event held in October 2018 that invited people worldwide to look at the sky and share their reflections via the Skyfie app

DAVID BRIN.name: DAVID BRIN
DAVID BRIN.description: David Brin is a science‑fiction author whose books are read and praised by Mae Jemison.

OCTAVIA BUTLER.name: OCTAVIA BUTLER
OCTAVIA BUTLER.description: Octavia Butler is a deceased science‑fiction writer whose works are discussed by Mae Jemison.

MARY DORIA RUSSELL.name: MARY DORIA RUSSELL
MARY DORIA RUSSELL.description: Mary Doria Russell is an American author best known for her acclaimed novel *The Sparrow*, a work that centers on the theme of first contact and the profound cultural and ethical questions that arise when humanity encounters an alien civilization. The novel has attracted attention from notable figures such as former astronaut Mae Jemison, who has spoken about the book and identified it as one of her personal favorites. In her discussions, Jemison highlights *The Sparrow* for its thoughtful exploration of the consequences of inter‑species contact and its nuanced portrayal of faith, science, and human resilience. Through this recognition, Mary Doria Russell’s contribution to speculative fiction and her ability to provoke deep reflection on the implications of contact with other intelligent life forms have been brought to a wider audience.

EP-28_BEHIND_THE_TECH_MAE-JEMISON.name: EP-28_BEHIND_THE_TECH_MAE-JEMISON
EP-28_BEHIND_THE_TECH_MAE-JEMISON.description: EP‑28_BEHIND_THE_TECH_MAE‑JEMISON is the twenty‑eighth episode of the **Behind the Tech** podcast, hosted by Kevin Scott. In this episode, Dr. Mae Jemison appears as the guest for a conversation that focuses on science education and the objectives of The Earth We Share program. The dialogue explores how to enhance science literacy among students, the value of engaging learning experiences, and the role of initiatives that empower young people to explore scientific concepts. Both Kevin Scott and Dr. Jemison share perspectives on fostering curiosity and providing resources that help future generations tackle global challenges. The episode highlights Dr. Jemison’s dedication to expanding access to quality STEM education and underscores the importance of communication between technology leaders and the broader public in advancing scientific understanding.

CHICAGO PUBLIC SCHOOLS.name: CHICAGO PUBLIC SCHOOLS
CHICAGO PUBLIC SCHOOLS.description: Chicago Public Schools (CPS) is the public school district that serves the city of Chicago, Illinois. It is the school system in which Mae Jemison, the physician, engineer, and former NASA astronaut, attended classes as a child. The district is also notable for having employed Dorothy Jemison, an educator who taught within Chicago Public Schools for a total of 25 years. Both women have notable connections to CPS, reflecting the district’s role in fostering talent across a range of fields. By encompassing a wide array of elementary, middle, and high schools, Chicago Public Schools provides educational opportunities to hundreds of thousands of students each year, contributing to the community’s intellectual and cultural development.

BROTHER.name: BROTHER
BROTHER.description: Mae Jemison's brother had science projects which allowed Mae to observe and participate in scientific activities at a young age

SISTER.name: SISTER
SISTER.description: Mae Jemison's sister had science projects which allowed Mae to observe and participate in scientific activities at a young age

EP-28.name: EP-28
EP-28.description: EP‑28 is a specific episode of the **Behind the Tech** podcast in which host Kevin Scott sits down for a conversation with former NASA astronaut, physician, and educator Mae Jemison. The episode centers on a wide‑ranging interview where Jemison shares insights from her career in space exploration, science, and technology advocacy, providing listeners with a personal look at her experiences and perspectives on the future of innovation. As with other installments of the series, EP‑28 offers an in‑depth discussion that blends technical topics with broader societal considerations, making it a notable addition to the Behind the Tech catalog.

FIND WHERE THE WIND GOES.name: FIND WHERE THE WIND GOES
FIND WHERE THE WIND GOES.description: This is a book written by Mae Jemison being re-released, about following clues throughout life and taking risks.

DOROTHY JEMISON.name: DOROTHY JEMISON
DOROTHY JEMISON.description: Dorothy Jemison was a dedicated educator who spent 25 years teaching in the Chicago Public Schools, where she became known for her commitment to students and to the community. She is also widely recognized as the mother of Dr. Mae Jemison, the physician and former NASA astronaut who became the first African‑American woman to travel in space. In honor of her lifelong contributions to education and her inspirational influence on her daughter and countless others, the Dorothy Jemison Foundation for Excellence was established and named after her. The foundation continues to promote excellence in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) education, reflecting the values and passion for learning that Dorothy Jemison embodied throughout her teaching career. Her legacy lives on through both her family’s achievements and the foundation’s programs that support students and educators across the United States.

DOROTHY JEMISON FOUNDATION FOR EXCELLENC.name: DOROTHY JEMISON FOUNDATION FOR EXCELLENCE
DOROTHY JEMISON FOUNDATION FOR EXCELLENC.description: The Dorothy Jemison Foundation for Excellence is a nonprofit organization that honors Dorothy Jemison by supporting innovative educational initiatives. Established by Dr. Mae Jemison, the foundation operates The Earth We Share program, a flagship experiential learning platform that provides hands‑on science education for students and equips teachers with research‑based training methods. The foundation’s mission centers on fostering excellence through immersive, experiential education, empowering learners and educators alike to address global challenges. By combining rigorous academic content with real‑world applications, the Dorothy Jemison Foundation for Excellence seeks to cultivate the next generation of innovative leaders.

LOS ANGELES UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT.name: LOS ANGELES UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT
LOS ANGELES UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT.description:
LOS ANGELES UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT (LAUSD) is a large public school district serving the city of Los Angeles. It has been actively seeking partnerships that enhance students’ learning experiences beyond the traditional classroom. One notable collaboration is with The Earth We Share, an initiative that provides experiential, hands‑on summer learning opportunities focused on sustainability, science, and global citizenship. LAUSD entered into this partnership to give its students access to immersive educational experiences that complement the district’s curriculum.

The collaboration with The Earth We Share was structured as a multi‑year effort. Over the course of four consecutive summers, the district and the program worked together to deliver a series of workshops, field projects, and leadership activities designed to foster environmental stewardship and interdisciplinary problem‑solving skills among participating students. This four‑year engagement allowed LAUSD to refine the program’s delivery, expand the number of schools involved, and measure the long‑term impact on student engagement and academic outcomes.

The Earth We Share is closely associated with the foundation led by former NASA astronaut and physician Dr. Mae Jemison. In practice, LAUSD’s partnership with the program effectively aligns the district with Dr. Jemison’s foundation, which shares the mission of empowering youth through experiential education. Consequently, the collaboration can also be described as a partnership between LAUSD and Mae Jemison’s foundation for a four‑year summer program that emphasizes learning by doing, critical thinking, and real‑world applications of scientific concepts.

Through these combined efforts, LOS ANGELES UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT has provided its students with unique opportunities to explore topics such as renewable energy, climate science, and community advocacy, while also gaining exposure to the leadership ideals embodied by Dr. Jemison. The sustained, multi‑year nature of the program underscores the district’s commitment to integrating high‑impact, experiential learning into its broader educational strategy.

SUMMER PROGRAM.name: SUMMER PROGRAM
SUMMER PROGRAM.description: The SUMMER PROGRAM is a four‑year, nonresidential summer program operating within the Los Angeles Unified School District (LAUSD) that focuses on experiential education. Run in partnership with LAUSD, the program offers hands‑on, experiential learning opportunities for participants during the summer months while allowing students to return home each day, as it does not provide residential accommodations. Its four‑year duration reflects a sustained commitment to delivering structured, experiential curricula aligned with the district’s educational objectives. By collaborating with LAUSD, the SUMMER PROGRAM leverages district resources, facilities, and expertise to enhance the overall impact of its summer offerings.

FOUR-WEEK RESIDENTIAL PROGRAM.name: FOUR-WEEK RESIDENTIAL PROGRAM
FOUR-WEEK RESIDENTIAL PROGRAM.description: The original four‑week residential component of The Earth We Share

DAY-LONG PROGRAMS.name: DAY-LONG PROGRAMS
DAY-LONG PROGRAMS.description: DAY-LONG PROGRAMS are international one‑day sessions organized as part of The Earth We Share initiative. They also function as international programs conducted in different countries as part of The Earth We Share expansion, delivering immersive, full‑day experiences that focus on sustainability, environmental stewardship, and cross‑cultural collaboration. These programs bring together learners, educators, and community leaders from around the world to engage in hands‑on activities, dialogues, and workshops that align with the overarching goals of The Earth We Share. By operating in multiple nations, DAY-LONG PROGRAMS help extend the reach of The Earth We Share, fostering a global network of advocates committed to building a more resilient and equitable planet.

EXPERIENTIAL EDUCATION.name: EXPERIENTIAL EDUCATION
EXPERIENTIAL EDUCATION.description: A teaching methodology emphasized in The Earth We Share programs where students learn by solving problems and working in teams

DANIELA RUS.name: DANIELA RUS
DANIELA RUS.description:
Daniela Rus is a world‑renowned robotics researcher, professor, and the first female director of the MIT Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL). A MacArthur Fellow (2002) and a member of the National Academy of Engineers, she was awarded the 2017 Engelberger Robotics Award by the Robotics Industries Association and serves as a U.S. expert member of the Global Partnership on AI. Her work bridges robotics, artificial intelligence, and distributed systems, with a focus on creating machines that are safe, adaptable, and beneficial to humanity.

**Research Themes**  
Rus is celebrated for integrating the “body and brain” of machines, a philosophy that underpins her research on soft robotics, human‑centric machines, and dexterous manipulation. She develops algorithms for autonomous behavior, including the well‑known “coffee challenge,” which tests a robot’s ability to perform fine‑grained tasks. Her laboratory has pioneered the **robot compiler**—a platform that enables rapid programming of diverse robots—alongside neural circuit policies that bring learning‑based control to physical systems. Rus emphasizes learning from the natural world to inspire new designs, advocating for soft robots, diverse materials, and applications that range from healthcare to environmental monitoring.

She is also a leading voice on the societal impact of AI. Rus discusses the carbon footprint of large machine‑learning models, the importance of sustainable AI, and the need for responsible deployment of technology. Her research explores how AI and computation can enhance healthcare, protect privacy, and inform technology policy, positioning her as a bridge between technical innovation and ethical stewardship.

**Leadership and Recognition**  
As director of CSAIL, Rus oversees a multidisciplinary group that advances the frontiers of robotics, AI, and mobile computing. Her contributions have earned her multiple prestigious honors, and she is frequently invited to speak at major venues, including the *Behind the Tech* podcast, where she shares insights on the future of machines, AI, and computational design.

**Personal Background**  
Born and raised in Romania, Rus spent part of her adolescence working in a factory that produced spare parts for locomotives—an experience that sparked her curiosity about how machines can interact with the physical world. She holds a deep appreciation for lifelong learning, which she nurtures through hobbies such as cooking, hosting dinner parties, skiing, scuba diving, and tennis. Rus cites Annabel Langbein’s cookbooks as a favorite resource, reflecting her blend of analytical rigor and everyday creativity.

**Overall Impact**  
Daniela Rus’s career exemplifies the convergence of pioneering robotics research, leadership in AI, and a commitment to sustainable, human‑centered technology. Her work continues to shape the trajectory of intelligent machines, ensuring they can assist with both cognitive and physical tasks while addressing the broader implications for society.

CSAIL.name: CSAIL
CSAIL.description:
CSAIL (Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory) is a prominent research laboratory located within the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT). It is one of the world’s largest and most influential research centers in computing, bringing together a community of roughly 125 faculty members and about 1,300 researchers, students, and staff. The laboratory’s research agenda is broad, with particular emphasis on robotics and artificial intelligence, although its work spans many other areas of computer science and engineering.

As an integral part of MIT, CSAIL operates as a hub for interdisciplinary collaboration, fostering innovation in fields such as machine learning, computer vision, natural language processing, human‑computer interaction, and autonomous systems. Its size and reputation enable it to attract top talent from around the globe, making it a driving force behind many breakthroughs that shape both academic research and industry practice.

The laboratory is led by Daniela Rus, a renowned scientist whose vision guides CSAIL’s strategic direction and research initiatives. Under her leadership, CSAIL continues to push the boundaries of what computers can do, aiming to create intelligent systems that can perceive, reason, and act in complex environments. Rus’s stewardship underscores the lab’s commitment to foundational advances in AI and robotics as well as the translation of these advances into real‑world applications.

In summary, CSAIL is a flagship research institution at MIT, distinguished by its large membership, extensive focus on robotics and artificial intelligence, and its status as a leading global center for computing research, all under the direction of Daniela Rus.

NATIONAL ACADEMY OF ENGINEERS.name: NATIONAL ACADEMY OF ENGINEERS
NATIONAL ACADEMY OF ENGINEERS.description: The National Academy of Engineers (NAE) is a prestigious, independent, nonprofit organization in the United States that serves as one of the nation’s leading bodies for engineering expertise, policy advice, and the recognition of outstanding engineering achievements. Election to the NAE is considered among the highest professional honors for engineers, and members are selected for their contributions to technological innovation, leadership, and the advancement of engineering knowledge. Among its distinguished membership is Daniela Rus, a renowned computer scientist and robotics researcher, who has been recognized for her pioneering work in autonomous systems, distributed robotics, and the development of programmable materials. Her inclusion in the NAE underscores the academy’s commitment to fostering interdisciplinary breakthroughs and representing the breadth of engineering excellence across academia, industry, and government. The NAE provides independent, evidence‑based guidance on complex engineering and technological challenges facing the nation, and it works to promote engineering education, public welfare, and the sustainable development of technology. By uniting leaders from diverse engineering fields, the National Academy of Engineers continues to shape the future of engineering research, practice, and policy in the United States.

ROBOTICS INDUSTRIES ASSOCIATION.name: ROBOTICS INDUSTRIES ASSOCIATION
ROBOTICS INDUSTRIES ASSOCIATION.description: The Robotics Industries Association (RIA) is an industry organization that represents and supports companies and professionals within the robotics sector. In 2017, the association honored Daniela Rus by awarding her the prestigious Engelberger Robotics Award, recognizing her significant contributions to robotics research and technology. The award underscores the Robotics Industries Association’s role in celebrating innovation and leadership within the robotics community.

MACARTHUR FELLOWSHIP.name: MACARTHUR FELLOWSHIP
MACARTHUR FELLOWSHIP.description: Prestigious award received by Daniela Rus in 2002 as a MacArthur Fellow

ENGELBERGER ROBOTICS AWARD.name: ENGELBERGER ROBOTICS AWARD
ENGELBERGER ROBOTICS AWARD.description: Robotics award given to Daniela Rus in 2017 by the Robotics Industries Association

EPISODE 40.description: The 40th episode of the Behind the Tech podcast featuring Daniela Rus

MIT COMPUTER SCIENCE AND AI LAB.name: MIT COMPUTER SCIENCE AND AI LAB
MIT COMPUTER SCIENCE AND AI LAB.description: MIT Computer Science and AI Lab (CSAIL) is a renowned research laboratory within the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) that concentrates on computer science and artificial intelligence. The lab is recognized for its broad research portfolio spanning algorithms, machine learning, robotics, natural language processing, computer vision, and related fields. As an academic research institution, CSAIL fosters interdisciplinary collaboration among faculty, graduate students, and post‑doctoral researchers. Among its faculty is Daniela Rus, a leading figure in robotics and AI whose work is based at the lab. CSAIL’s mission is to advance the frontier of computer science and AI through innovative research, education, and technology transfer, making it a central hub for both theoretical and applied studies at MIT.

MASSACHUSETTS INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY.name: MASSACHUSETTS INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY
MASSACHUSETTS INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY.description: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) is the university that houses the Computer Science and AI Lab

NEW HAMPSHIRE.description: New Hampshire is a U.S. state where the 1956 Dartmouth Summer AI Workshop took place in the woods.
